state.next-action: ROUND 4 FULLY REVIEWED + MASSIVE SITE/FRAMEWORK BACKLOG CAPTURED. All 4 round-4 builds have rubric reviews: GAD v9 (0.05 legacy, rate-limited), GAD v10 (0.02 rubric, api-interrupted, novel title screen but nothing beyond), Bare v5 (TBD — user still to play), Emergent v4 (0.805 rubric aggregate). EXPERIMENT-LOG.md Round 4 section rewritten with real data (was "TBD"). v5 requirements addendum drafted (`evals/_v5-requirements-addendum.md`, 12 requirements R-v5.01..12). Three new decisions: gad-68 (emergent-evolution working hypothesis — projects are emergent, skills must be tailored + merged + rehearsed against foundational pool; combines freedom + CSH + Ralph Wiggum + repomirror observations), gad-69 (programmatic evaluation priority — minimize agent-self-report dependency, every metric must answer "can this be collected programmatically?"), gad-70 (Anthropic skills guide is canonical reference — three testing layers: triggering/functional/performance-comparison; three categories: doc-creation/workflow/mcp-enhancement). HUGE SITE/FRAMEWORK BACKLOG captured as tasks 84-90: (84) IA refactor with nav dropdowns + keyword search + predictable routing pattern, (85) skills directory with copy-to-clipboard SKILL.md display modelled after mcpmarket but in our style, (86) /security page covering skill security + coding-agent attacks + typosquatting + our security eval approach, (87) /glossary + jargon tooltip system (greenfield/brownfield/etc) with a Term component backed by glossary.json, (88) /roadmap round timeline with per-round transparency including shortcomings + mitigations + data-provenance docs per chart, (89) plan codex vs claude-code comparison eval (runner field on gad.json, side-by-side compare page) — inspired by openclaw's coding-agent skill pattern, (90) ASSUMPTIONS.md documenting target users + workflows. Phase 27 programmatic-eval gaps audit queued as task 83. NEXT AGENT SESSION PRIORITY ORDER: (1) ASSUMPTIONS.md + GAPS.md audit (informs everything else), (2) IA refactor plan before shipping more pages, (3) /glossary + Term component (unblocks tooltip-wrapping everywhere), (4) /roadmap timeline with round 4 honest writeup, (5) skills directory with copy, (6) /security page, (7) investigate HTTP 529 + promote v5 requirements + retry GAD v11 serially, (8) codex-vs-claude comparison implementation. Do NOT run round 5 against v4 — v5 promotion is a hard prerequisite.
